review_title: Great for a Non-techie like me
description: I've loved using Leadpages because it is simple, intuitive, and creates landing pages that look great. I recommend Leadpages to others for these reasons.
author: Michael D.
pros: Leadpages is so easy to use. I'm NOT a techie and Leadpages allows me to create landing pages in less than 5 minutes.
cons: I've used Leadpages for over 5 years and it does exactly what I need it to do. The updated look of the sites is great and I cannot think of anything that I don't like.

description: I'm creating a lot of sign-up forms or sales pages to show the materials I have for sale and opt-ins for my email list. I also created a page that is like link-tree which is on my company's Instagram profile. I liked creating my own because I found link-tree to be fussy sometimes and would randomly stop working. Never had a problem with my leadpage one though!
author: Rachel M.
pros: I find this software super easy to use and functional. It does what I need it to do and the more I use it, the more little tricks and design things I figure out that make my materials POP. I love the tool that examines the lead to give advice on what I can make better (the lead optimizer tool). That's super helpful and informative. I'm the only one on my team that uses leadpages. I feel like I get to get feedback on my materials before I go live with something. And I LOVE that I can easily refresh my updates/publish and I don't have to update a link anywhere. I can quickly make changes with push of a button!
cons: Sometimes I design a page and when I go to preview it, the aspect ratios have changed and I have to go back in and redesign. For instance, a header logo with some text below it may fit perfectly within the box at the top of the page. When I preview it, the logo/text doesn't optimize for the new window (like phone or tablet) and I have to keep resizing to figure out what will fit. To me there should be a standard width of frame that will auto-fix the size when changing to phone or tablet. It would be nice to also have more drag and drop functionality. Like that an image would fit ANYWHERE in a section I create (like wix or weebly does with their websites). Sometimes I have to do a lot of pre-design art work when I'm creating something. And then it doesn't even allow for a text box to fit where I want it to.

author: John L.
pros: The name is nice I guess. Easy to remember. Pleasant demeanor of their support staff, but they "couldn't find" my ccount when I asked to cancel, and then continued to charge me for 2 years.
cons: Did not work any better than what I could do myself, did not save me any time, in fact I wasted time on their system, and THEY OVERCHARGED AND WOULDN'T CANCEL. ... like Comcast.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: It is a very good option to make landig pages and capture leads, the templates are very nice and you have for all uses, they are quick to download and good lead capture.
author: Patricia A.
pros: What I liked the most were the templates, the ease of publication, the easy editing and the a/b test.
cons: What I liked least was that in some things although you should be able to do them, the modification of templates failed, the gallery seemed good but limited.

review_title: I’ve tried them all, and Leadpages still wins!
description: Leadpages has changed my life! Literally! I use it daily to support my business, but more importantly I use it to sell my copywriting and design services as a contractor and consultant. As such, I’ve worked with a ton of other page builders. While a few (like unbounce) have caught my attention with their cool widgets and mobile builders, I always come back to Leadpages because of the great price, fantastic support and knowledge that the product team is always putting out new things. Also, Leadpages is just so simple to use!
author: Lacy B.
pros: My favorite thing about Leadpages is that the product is always being updated with new, helpful features. Since the reorganization of the dashboard, it is very user friendly and intuitive. For the most part, all of the major functional features I need to make high converting pages are there. I love how easy integrations are, and I absolutely LOVE that I can copy sections, use section templates and easily alter page templates to my liking.
cons: I have been saying for years now that there needs to be more functionality for images. An on page image resizer would be fantastic. It would also be very helpful to be able to insert image carousels. I would also love to see a separate mobile builder. Design is very important when it comes to conversions, and I think some of the most wanted features are missing.

description: Very popular, great conversion rate, a little pricey. I would definitely invest in Leadpages as the business began to turn a profit.
author: Tanya G.
pros: Leadpages has plenty of great templates and styles. You can customize a lot of areas. You can also brand color and logo all your pages.
cons: The cost was a bit high for what I was going to use it for plus it took a little time to learn. If you were doing more sales pages with higher volume sales it would be really useful.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Good just not as good as others
description: Overall it worked. It could do the job. It's just not the best out there
author: Charles A.
pros: I liked that I could easily make landing pages.
cons: It lacked some of the functionality and I found it to be a lot slower than other options out there.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Amazing website creating platform, not many know about! NO more WP!
description: My overall experience with Leadpages has been positive: it’s simple, easy to use, and makes website management straightforward for me. I can set up pages quickly without complexity, keep everything organized, and update content with minimal effort.
author: Michael K.
pros: managing multiple websites is easy, efficient, secure, scalable, cost‑effective, and reliably maintainable across teams.
cons: No new updates , though lately the platform has been improving with many new ones, steadily with fixes and smoother performance.

description: We have overall been very pleased with the platform. It has met almost all of our wants and needs as a small business.
author: Kristin L.
pros: We have used leadpages for years. We like that it is a reasonable expense for our team to create opt in pages. We have found the customer support to be responsive when needed. It has made it easy to create beautiful and intuitive website pages.
cons: I wish there was a drag and drop feature. I also wish it was easier to customize the exact layout/size of things. 9/10 we can make it work, but occasionally we want something really specific and cannot get there with lead pages.

description: Love the product at the time I used it..
author: Marcus G.
pros: Ease of use, not to hard to use once you learn it
cons: Did have any problems with it at the time of use
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Great complete tool, a bit pricey
description: Helped me generate my first leads with beautiful landing pages
author: Korney M.
pros: Very complete, many features and frequent webinars that showed how to use the tools and the new features
cons: The cost : it was quite pricey especially for a new business.

review_title: Super Intuitive Interface and Easy to Use
description: I operate a non-profit organization and we run a school and a clinic. I use Leadpages for BOTH of those websites, and for our main organization website. I also changed all of our landing pages to Leadpages. I remember reading once while I was teaching myself SEO that every page should be a landing page; meaning every page should have a single focus, and a call to action where you collect an email in return for something so that you can continue communicating with everyone who visits your site. For a long time I tried to create landing pages on my own in wordpress, but Leadpages is just unparalleled. I even use an email software, ConvertKit, that has landing pages, but Leadpages is just so much better on multiple levels that it is worth it to pay separately for landing page software. their analytics, stunning design and super easy (almost never buggy) software just makes me happy - literally. When software is easy, I feel like I can express my creativity more, it feels like my work is more fun, and this is worth it's weight in gold!
author: Aimee S.
pros: It's easy to use & lightweight. I know this may sound silly, but it is FUN to use. I want to create new pages, because the templates are so beautiful and I always get inspired by what they have in the templates. I work online all day & I need software that is easy, and inspires me, Leadpages does that.
cons: Honestly, I can't think of anything I do not like. I always wanted to use Leadpages, I even had an account in the past, but stopped using it, because I was not able to commit financially to the monthly payment. However, every single penny I pay for it has come back to me in new students, clients and leads since I started, so there is really no reason not to use it.

review_title: Lovely landing pages, but missing a few key elements
description: Beautiful landing pages that are way easier to make than I could do on my own.
author: heather T.
pros: Everyone loves Leadpages, right? When you think about good looking landing pages, Leadpages is who comes to mind. Gorgeous templates, easy drag-and-drop editor, integrations with WordPress so you can host your pages on your site, and for the more expensive plans you can integrate Stripe products and payments. Which leads me to what I don't like...
cons: I'm frustrated that the coupon functionality with Stripe doesn't work in Leadpages. I use coupons as a way for other bloggers to sell my events, and I track their usage by who used which coupons. It's not the most sophisticated way to do it, but it works. But even though you can make coupons in Stripe, the functionality isn't supported in Leadpages. So I upgraded, and paid the extra so that it was a smooth experience for customers, but when the coupons didn't work I had to change. My workaround was to put a Shopify Buy Button on the landing page, embedding the html code. It's not as pretty, but it works.

author: Ismaël Huberson C.
pros: I use Leadpages for my business because it is so easy to use and allows me to create landing pages, pop-ups and more with their simple drag and drop builder. Their customer service is also very good and always prompt in getting back to me. They have a full team of experts that you can contact anytime you need help including training. You can also get a free trial to try out their services before buying. I highly recommend them.
cons: Leadpages is a nice tool to create landing pages.

review_title: Poor support and difficult to use
author: Johnny S.
pros: I like the overall appearance of the leadpages. I like that it can be used with facebook but have not tried this part of the software.
cons: I've been paying these guys per month for several years now for just one leadpage. I never call their support center. I called to inquire about sales but had to leave a vmail and the response I received came to my email below... "Phone support is currently only available to Advanced Subscribers, as we just don't have the staffing power to provide it to everyone. More about support options here: How to Contact Technical Support" The fact that I was actually calling about sales and I can't speak to a real person is frustrating. Furthermore I work in engineering with software and even I found it difficult to figure out how to do basic things with their platform. Its like they have all these ideas but their engineering team doesn't take the time to consider how the features will flow on their UI to be intuitive. The system is so complicated that it requires for you to upgrade your plan to receive phone support for them to help you. There are companies like mailchimp that don't even offer phone support however the difference is they offer a simple mail template platform, however leadpages is way to difficult to use and in return not offer phone support. Bottom line is they treat their lower plan users like 2nd class citizens unless you pay them a high amount monthly, they will probably give you the same kind of support I received. I'm currently looking at other leadpage solutions I found on google and found a couple cheaper options that offer phone support.

description: At first, I wanted to implement decent-looking 2-step opt-in forms, where users could click a link and bring up a pop-up. In 2014, that was surprisingly challenging to do. I've scaled back from that strategy and the extra javascript leadpages loads, but still use the software to host several one-off landing pages, custom thank-you pages / digital product sales pages, and even a custom 404 page.
author: Nick L.
pros: I was a reluctant LeadPages customer in 2014, but it turned out to be a great purchase. Within 3 months, my email list had tripled. Within 12 months, it had 12x'd. It was a big inflection point for my business. I like the pre-built templates (always easier than starting from scratch), and how fast it is to build pages. There have been a few issues of down time, but overall it's very reliable. I recently started testing the countdown timer and Stripe integration, which has been a cool new way to sell digital products.
cons: Mainly the price. I'm always tempted by other landing page builders on AppSumo, but at this point LeadPages has the SaaS tentacles wrapped around me pretty tight. It would take a lot of work to undo it and switch to another tool.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Building pages it SO easy. There's very little learning curve
cons: They stopped supporting meta refreshes, didn't tell anyone about it and refuse to fix the bug. They say it's a feature. Customer support just sends scripted responses. No customer care at all.

description: I am satisfied and would recommend it to anyone looking for a simple way to get a landing page, full website or a way to collect quality leads. As the name suggests, it is a great resource to build site pages, promote it in Facebook and other social media and collect quality leads while tracking analytics for each page.
author: Sujee K.
pros: It is absolutely clean, beautiful and easy to use (if you are not working with WordPress). I like the drag and drop feature and how fast you can update your pages. I use it with WordPress but it took some time to get used to with that. They've recently added a top bar feature so make announcements on any page and you can integrate the pages with 3rd party apps like MailChimp! Lead generation is simple and you can redirect users after signing up to any page you like. The way you use their software is only limited by your creativity and imagination. I use it not only as a landing page but I host my entire site on it as well!
cons: The software is not perfect. I use a separate messenger service called 'Intercom' that breaks whenever I click on a button generated by Leadpages - those two services are not compatible. I reached out to customer support but they had no solution for it. Also, the options you have to build your page is limited - for example, you can't include slides and the html widget is not perfect.

description: Great experience with Leadpages overall. It's easy to make really, really nice landing pages that look professional. My number one complaint with sales page builders is often that they are hard to make them look the like the "example page" that is given - however, I never found that to be the case with Leadpages.
author: Michelle G.
pros: Loved how easy it is to create landing pages that are streamlined, beautiful, and mobile-optimized. Sometimes sales page software (or email opt-in boxes) look clunky or it's hard to put together something professional looking. Not the case with Leadpages.
cons: I used pages for about 2 years and it integrated beautifully with my website and email platform. Then I switched email platforms to one that has landing pages included. So I don't really have any complaints about Leadpages, just that it was one more thing to attach (along with Zapier) to my email and website and it seemed simpler to go without it. That doesn't mean my landing pages are nice as they used to be, though :)

description: We have been able to load hundreds of lead magnets within a few weeks and delivering them to prospective clients in a "set it and forget it" kind of way. And we can easily implement more, add pop-ups to our website, and deliver a professional experience to our prospects without a lot of work on our end. We can do it in-house within minutes, make changes easily, without the back-and-forth of hiring someone to do it for us.
author: Jamie M.
pros: Leadpages got us up and running, delivering lead magnets in a very short amount of time. Within minutes you can create your first item, and that saved time and money over hiring a third party to develop a custom solution.
cons: I wish there was better organization of the lead magnets themselves. We have hundreds of items now and it is extremely tedious to go through each one if we need to make a change. There is also not an easy way to search and sort through to find "the one" you are looking for from the main list.

review_title: Leadpages, great builder, without too much fuzz
description: REALLY good. I use at least three builders at the moment, and for quick and easy optin pages only, Leadpages sets a standard in FAST, EASY. It may look and appear simple, but that's its power. I am really pleased to use it.
author: Rune E.
pros: I like that it is easy to use. It is clutter free, unlike some other contendors in the space. They have several options and the interface is sleek to use no matter the plan you are on. It is fast to create a site. They have a panel on the left when you edit your pages, which is not overstuffed at all. Making it a n easy to understand and to get going with within MINUTES. Like you can have a page up and running REALLY fast. Fastest builder I have used in over 10 years, so it is stripped for the un-necessary gadgets and apps.
cons: While the interface is good and the builder is fast, I guess the only thing I can say I kinda wanted MORE of, is the ability to create a full on website with my plan. With the "baby plan", not sure what it's called right now, you can create optin pages, mostly. If you go one plan up, you can add payment options and more bells and whistles.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It’s a critical part of our business. In fact, I wonder if our business would exist today without it.
author: Amin A.
pros: I’m not a coder, so I value the ability to create lead pages from scratch using the intuitive drag and drop system created by Lead Pages. Additionally, given that so much of our traffic is from mobile devices, I enjoy that Leadpages allows us to customize the page for desktop and mobile users. Finally, our Company uses Drip, and I like that Leadpages is integrated with the Drip CRM systems.
cons: Sometimes, especially during “busy” times during the day, the Lead Pages system will take a while to load. Additionally, there are some 3rd party apps (e.g. Kickoff Labs) that are not easily integrated with Leadpages.

author: Munema I.
pros: I would highly recommend Leadpages to all those looking for a digital tool to help them with building a landing page, popup forms, alter bars and more! The Leadpages template library is my favorite feature because it's an excellent way to give the page a feel and look I would like it to have. There seems to be pretty much a template for each circumstance, which is a very good thing to know.
cons: Templates are amazing and should be customized... othewise, the webpage built by user A is going to look pretty much the same, from an aesthetic point of view, as that of user B. After having used Leadpages for a while, you are going to start to recognize a specific template being created by someone else

review_title: Good for Landing pages, often buggy
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Leadpages allow you to connect unlimited domains, so you are able to host unlimited websites, so to say. That makes it quite useful for people with several ventures that need landing pages for several domains. The editor interface is not difficult to use and it allows you to customize the content in the page to an acceptable degree. You can save Page colors to be able to reuse them over the page, avoiding inconsistency. Their new Checkout form is easy to use and provides a really fast way to accept credit card payments, small merchants would love this.
cons: Leadpages interfaces was often buggy, saving the changes sometimes failed and the Leadmeter is sometimes failing to guess properly, giving frequent hints on things that shouldn't decrease the ability of the page to generate leads. The AB testing was a good idea, is easy to use but when I decided the winner between the two I lost all the analytics from the old page, sometimes showing the number but unable to access the reports. I found that the analytics are often misleading, counting clicks as conversion making the revenue reports not accurate. Integrations with third-party services were also failing sometimes. Overall I think the system needs more work in the engineering to make it more robust and stable.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Great experience dealing with customer support every time I need them
author: Fitor K.
pros: Quick response and very easy to contact
cons: Easy to contact customer support and patience to understand all issues
